_comment: Registry tweaks for Windows 11 Explorer/Taskbar UI de-annoyance. Same schema as privacy_keys.json. Categories: classic_menu (Win11 right-click revert), taskbar, search, file_visibility, theme, copilot_ui. explorer_conflicts.json must be checked first — if StartAllBack/ExplorerPatcher/Start11/OpenShell is installed, skip classic_menu.

keys.name: (Default)
keys.note: Empty-string InprocServer32 override forces Win11 to fall back to the classic Win10 right-click context menu. Requires Explorer restart. Threatened by Microsoft — verify at runtime.

keys.name: TaskbarAl
keys.note: Taskbar alignment: 0 = left (Win10 style), 1 = center (Win11 default)

keys.name: SearchboxTaskbarMode
keys.note: 0 = hidden, 1 = icon only, 2 = search box, 3 = search box with icon

keys.name: Hidden
keys.note: 1 = show hidden files, 2 = do not show. Ask user first — many users prefer default hidden.

keys.name: FullPath
keys.note: Show full path in Explorer title bar / address bar

keys.name: ShowSuperHidden
keys.note: Show protected OS files. Ask user — advanced/dangerous.

keys.name: AppsUseLightTheme
keys.note: 0 = dark, 1 = light. Ask user — subjective preference.

keys.name: TaskbarMn
keys.note: Chat / Microsoft Teams icon off. Ask user — some use Teams personal chat from the taskbar as their entry point to Teams itself. Moved to ask_user 2026-07-07.

keys.name: ShowTaskViewButton
keys.note: Task View button off. Ask user — many users navigate virtual desktops with this button; silently hiding it breaks their workflow. Moved to ask_user 2026-07-07.

notes.reasoning_2026-07-07: TaskbarMn (Chat) and ShowTaskViewButton were previously in apply_silently categories (copilot_ui + taskbar). Post-BT-pairing incident audit flagged them: both are muscle-memory Windows UX elements the user notices when hidden. Split into their own ask_user categories so they're each individually promptable and the previously-silent categories stay silent for the actually-invisible changes (Widgets policy, taskbar alignment, search box).
